book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
A Synod consisting of One Hundred and Fifty Bishops meets at Constantinople. The Decrees passed. Ordination of Nectarius.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: A Synod consisting of One Hundred and Fifty Bishops meets at Constantinople. The Decrees passed. Ordination of Nectarius.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The emperor making no delay summoned a Synod of the prelates of his own faith, in order that he might establish the Nicene Creed, and appoint a bishop of Constantinople: and inasmuch as he was not without hope that he might win the Macedonians over to his own views, he invited those who presided over that sect to be present also.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: There met therefore on this occasion of the Homoousian party, Timothy from Alexandria, Cyril from Jerusalem, who at that time recognized the doctrine of homoousion, having retracted his former opinion; Melitius from Antioch, he having arrived there previously to assist at the installation of Gregory; Ascholius also from Thessalonica, and many others, amounting in all to one hundred and fifty.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Of the Macedonians, the leaders were Eleusius of Cyzicus, and Marcian of Lampsacus; these with the rest, most of whom came from the cities of the Hellespont, were thirty-six in number.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Accordingly they were assembled in the month of May, under the consulate of Eucharius and Evagrius, and the emperor used his utmost exertions, in conjunction with the bishops who entertained similar sentiments to his own, to bring over Eleusius and his adherents to his own side.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They were reminded of the deputation they had sent by Eustathius to Liberius then bishop of Rome; that they had of their own accord not long before entered into promiscuous communion with the orthodox; and the inconsistency and fickleness of their conduct was represented to them, in now attempting to subvert the faith which they once acknowledged, and professed agreement with the catholics in.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But they paying little heed alike to admonitions and reproofs, chose rather to maintain the Arian dogma, than to assent to the ‘homoousian’ doctrine.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Having made this declaration, they departed from Constantinople; moreover they wrote to their partisans in every city, and charged them by no means to harmonize with the creed of the Nicene Synod.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The bishops of the other party remaining at Constantinople, entered into a consultation about the ordination of a bishop; for Gregory, as we have before said, had resigned that see, and was preparing to return to Nazianzus.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now there was a person named Nectarius, of a senatorial family, mild and gentle in his manners, and admirable in his whole course of life, although he at that time bore the office of proctor.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This man was seized upon by the people, and elected to the episcopate, and was ordained accordingly by one hundred and fifty bishops then present.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The same prelates moreover published a decree, prescribing ‘that the bishop of Constantinople should have the next prerogative of honor after the bishop of Rome, because that city was New Rome.’

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They also again confirmed the Nicene Creed.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Then too patriarchs were constituted, and the provinces distributed, so that no bishop might exercise any jurisdiction over other churches out of his own diocese: for this had been often indiscriminately done before, in consequence of the persecutions.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: To Nectarius therefore was allotted the great city and Thrace.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Helladius, the successor of Basil in the bishopric of Cæsarea in Cappadocia, obtained the patriarchate of the diocese of Pontus in conjunction with Gregory Basil’s brother, bishop of Nyssa in Cappadocia, and Otreïus bishop of Melitina in Armenia.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: To Amphilochius of Iconium and Optimus of Antioch in Pisidia, was the Asiatic diocese assigned.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The superintendence of the churches throughout Egypt was committed to Timothy of Alexandria.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: On Pelagius of Laodicea, and Diodorus of Tarsus, devolved the administration of the churches of the East; without infringement however on the prerogatives of honor reserved to the Antiochian church, and conferred on Melitius then present.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: They further decreed that as necessity required it, the ecclesiastical affairs of each province should be managed by a Synod of the province.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: These arrangements were confirmed by the emperor’s approbation.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Such was the result of this Synod.
